items.title: 2026 SpaceX IPO: Investors Want to Buy a Space Stock, but They'll Get an ISP Instead
items.author: Rich Smith
items.description: SpaceX announced a 2026 IPO, but the company has evolved into primarily an Internet Service Provider rather than a pure space company. With approximately 70% of revenue coming from Starlink satellite internet services compared to traditional space operations, investors expecting a space company investment may actually be buying into an ISP business model with significantly higher profit margins than traditional aerospace companies.
